queries.answer:
**Best overall restaurant in Cardiff: _Gorse_ in Pontcanna.**  
It’s Cardiff’s **first and currently only Michelin-starred restaurant**, serving modern British/Welsh tasting menus with a strong local-produce focus. Michelin lists it as **One Star: high quality cooking**, and Time Out ranks it **#1 in Cardiff**, calling it “the finest Cardiff has to offer.” ([guide.michelin.com](https://guide.michelin.com/en/south-glamorgan/cardiff/restaurant/gorse?utm_source=openai))

**Good alternatives depending on what you want:**

- **Special occasion / fine dining:** **Heaneys** — seasonal tasting menus, great wine list. ([timeout.com](https://www.timeout.com/cardiff/restaurants/best-restaurants-in-cardiff))  
- **Ramen / casual but excellent:** **Matsudai Ramen** — Wales’s first dedicated ramen shop. ([timeout.com](https://www.timeout.com/cardiff/restaurants/best-restaurants-in-cardiff))  
- **Spanish grill / steak:** **Asador 44** — strong pick near the Principality Stadium. ([timeout.com](https://www.timeout.com/cardiff/restaurants/best-restaurants-in-cardiff))  
- **Indian:** **Purple Poppadom** — award-winning, Michelin-noted South Asian cooking. ([timeout.com](https://www.timeout.com/cardiff/restaurants/best-restaurants-in-cardiff))  

If you want **the best single pick**, book **Gorse**—but reserve well ahead because it’s small and popular.

If you mean **Cardiff, Wales**, the best “top-rated” pick depends on the rating source:

**Best overall / fine dining:** **Gorse** — Cardiff’s **only Michelin-starred restaurant**, serving Modern British food at **186–188 Kings Road, CF11 9DF**. Michelin lists it as **One Star: High quality cooking**, and describes it as an intimate, chef-led restaurant focused on the Welsh larder. ([guide.michelin.com](https://guide.michelin.com/en/south-glamorgan/cardiff/restaurant/gorse?utm_source=openai))

**Top on Tripadvisor-style diner rankings:** **Ciliegino Restaurant** appears at #1 on Tripadvisor’s Cardiff restaurant list, with a **4.8/5** rating; it’s an Italian restaurant. ([tripadvisor.com](https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g186460-Cardiff_South_Wales_Wales.html))

**Other strong choices:**  
- **Heaneys** — Michelin-listed, stylish modern restaurant in Pontcanna. ([guide.michelin.com](https://guide.michelin.com/gb/en/south-glamorgan/cardiff/restaurant/heaneys?utm_source=openai))  
- **Hiræth** — Michelin-listed, on Cowbridge Road East. ([guide.michelin.com](https://guide.michelin.com/us/en/south-glamorgan/cardiff/restaurant/hiraeth-1215955?utm_source=openai))  
- **Pasture Cardiff** — highly rated steakhouse/fine-dining option, shown with **4.8/5** on Tripadvisor. ([tripadvisor.com](https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g186460-Cardiff_South_Wales_Wales.html))

**My recommendation:** book **Gorse** if you want the most acclaimed/special-occasion restaurant; choose **Ciliegino** if you want a highly rated, more casual Italian meal.

**My top pick: Gorse** — best if you want a special, modern Welsh meal in Cardiff. It focuses on hyper-local Welsh produce and seasonal tasting menus, and Visit Wales notes it holds **a Michelin Star** and **three AA Rosettes**. ([visitwales.com](https://www.visitwales.com/things-do/food-and-drink/places-eat-and-drink/guide-eating-cardiff))

If you want alternatives:

- **Asador 44** — excellent for steak/Spanish-style charcoal cooking in the city centre; a strong choice for a date night or group dinner. ([visitwales.com](https://www.visitwales.com/things-do/food-and-drink/places-eat-and-drink/guide-eating-cardiff))  
- **The Potted Pig** — atmospheric spot in a former bank vault, with modern takes on Welsh food. ([visitwales.com](https://www.visitwales.com/things-do/food-and-drink/places-eat-and-drink/guide-eating-cardiff))  

**If you only book one:** go with **Gorse** for a memorable Cardiff dining experience.

queries.query: who are the best Restaurant in Cardiff
queries.answer:
Here are some of the **best restaurants in Cardiff**, depending on what you’re looking for:

| Restaurant | Best for | Area / vibe |
|---|---|---|
| **Gorse** | **Top fine dining / special occasion** | Pontcanna. Cardiff’s first and currently only Michelin-starred restaurant; modern British tasting menus focused on Welsh produce. ([guide.michelin.com](https://guide.michelin.com/gb/en/article/features/michelin-star-restaurants-in-wales?utm_source=openai)) |
| **Heaneys** | Creative tasting menu | Pontcanna/Canton. Run by Tommy Heaney; good for a polished but relaxed meal. ([visitwales.com](https://www.visitwales.com/things-do/food-and-drink/places-eat-and-drink/guide-eating-cardiff?utm_source=openai)) |
| **Hiraeth** | Modern Welsh cooking | Cowbridge Road East. Strong local-produce focus and cosy, refined feel. ([visitwales.com](https://www.visitwales.com/things-do/food-and-drink/places-eat-and-drink/guide-eating-cardiff?utm_source=openai)) |
| **Purple Poppadom** | Best Indian / modern curry night | Canton. Michelin-listed Indian restaurant known for layered spicing and dishes like tiffin sea bass. ([guide.michelin.com](https://guide.michelin.com/gb/en/south-glamorgan/cardiff/restaurant/purple-poppadom?utm_source=openai)) |
| **Asador 44** | Spanish grill, steak & seafood | City centre. Great if you want charcoal-grilled meat, fish and Spanish-style dining. ([visitcardiff.com](https://www.visitcardiff.com/2025/01/tripadvisors-top-rated-restaurants/?utm_source=openai)) |
| **Pasture** | Steak | City centre. Popular for high-quality cuts and a lively, premium steakhouse feel. ([visitcardiff.com](https://www.visitcardiff.com/2025/01/tripadvisors-top-rated-restaurants/?utm_source=openai)) |
| **Thomas by Tom Simmons** | French-influenced Welsh food | Pontcanna. Smart but comfortable; good for date night or a refined dinner. ([visitwales.com](https://www.visitwales.com/things-do/food-and-drink/places-eat-and-drink/guide-eating-cardiff?utm_source=openai)) |
| **Mesen** | Neighbourhood small plates | Rhiwbina. The Good Food Guide highlights it for relaxed contemporary cooking. ([thegoodfoodguide.co.uk](https://www.thegoodfoodguide.co.uk/where-to-eat/best-restaurants-in-cardiff?utm_source=openai)) |
| **Ogof** | Natural wine & seasonal plates | Kings Road/Pontcanna. Good for a more modern, low-key foodie meal. ([visitwales.com](https://www.visitwales.com/things-do/food-and-drink/places-eat-and-drink/guide-eating-cardiff?utm_source=openai)) |
| **The Potted Pig** | Classic Cardiff pick | City centre. European/British food in a former bank vault; a long-running favourite. ([tripadvisor.com](https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g186460-Cardiff_South_Wales_Wales.html?utm_source=openai)) |

**My quick picks:**  
- **Best overall:** Gorse  
- **Best steak:** Pasture or Asador 44  
- **Best Indian:** Purple Poppadom  
- **Best date night:** Heaneys or Thomas by Tom Simmons  
- **Best local Welsh feel:** Hiraeth  
- **Best casual foodie spot:** Ogof or Mesen

queries.answer:
Assuming you mean **Cardiff, Wales** — here are good **affordable** picks:

1. **Falafel Corner – Queen Street / Capitol Shopping Centre**  
   Best for a very cheap, filling meal. Large Lebanese wrap is listed at **£8**, large salad bowl **£8**, pitta **£7**, and a meal deal for two **£15**; good vegetarian/vegan option. ([weur-cdn.menuweb.menu](https://weur-cdn.menuweb.menu/storage/media/companies_menu_pdf/114644282/falafel-corner-cardiff-menu.pdf))

2. **Ffwrnes Pizza – Cardiff Central Market**  
   Great budget lunch spot for wood-fired pizza. Current menu shows pizzas from about **£8–£13**; e.g., Marinara **£8**, Margherita **£9**. It’s in Cardiff Market and typically daytime hours, so better for lunch than late dinner. ([ffwrnes.pizza](https://www.ffwrnes.pizza/?utm_source=openai))

3. **Tukka Tuk – Cardiff Market**  
   Good-value Indian/Keralan street food. Delivery/menu listings show build-your-own bowls from about **£4.15** and small plates around **£3.50–£6.60**. ([just-eat.co.uk](https://www.just-eat.co.uk/restaurants-tukka-tuk-canteen-cardiff/menu?utm_source=openai))

4. **Cafe Citta – Church Street**  
   A cosy Italian sit-down option in the centre; menu listings show many pasta/pizza dishes in a relatively affordable range, though prices may have changed so check before going. ([restaurantguru.com](https://restaurantguru.com/Cafe-Citta-Cardiff/menu?utm_source=openai))

**My quick pick:**  
- **Cheapest:** Falafel Corner  
- **Best casual lunch:** Ffwrnes Pizza  
- **Best sit-down:** Cafe Citta  

If you meant **Cardiff-by-the-Sea, California** instead of Cardiff, Wales, tell me and I’ll give you local options there.

Here are solid **Cardiff city centre restaurant** picks:

- **Asador 44** — best for a special meal: Spanish/Mediterranean, wood-fired cooking, great wine; on Quay Street. ([guide.michelin.com](https://guide.michelin.com/us/en/south-glamorgan/cardiff/restaurant/asador-44?utm_source=openai))  
- **The Potted Pig** — modern British/Welsh food in a quirky underground former bank vault; very central on High Street. ([thepottedpig.com](https://www.thepottedpig.com/contact/?utm_source=openai))  
- **Pasture Cardiff** — lively steakhouse near Cardiff Castle, good for meat, cocktails, and a buzzy atmosphere. ([pasturerestaurant.com](https://pasturerestaurant.com/locations/pasture-cardiff/?utm_source=openai))  
- **Bar 44** — Spanish tapas on Westgate Street; good for sharing plates and drinks. ([southwalesguide.com](https://www.southwalesguide.com/directory/bar-44-cardiff?utm_source=openai))  
- **Mowgli Street Food** — casual Indian street-food style option on Church Street. ([visitcardiff.com](https://www.visitcardiff.com/eat-drink/asian/?utm_source=openai))  

**My quick pick:**  
- **Date/special occasion:** Asador 44  
- **Welsh/local feel:** The Potted Pig  
- **Steak/night out:** Pasture  
- **Casual group meal:** Bar 44 or Mowgli  

Book ahead if it’s a Friday/Saturday or match/event day in Cardiff.

queries.query: where can I find a good Restaurant in Cardiff
queries.answer:
Here are some **good restaurant picks in Cardiff**, depending on what you’re after:

- **Best special occasion / fine dining:** **Gorse** in Pontcanna — Cardiff’s Michelin-starred modern British restaurant, focused on Welsh produce and tasting menus. Book ahead. ([guide.michelin.com](https://guide.michelin.com/us/en/south-glamorgan/cardiff/restaurant/gorse?utm_source=openai))  
- **Modern, stylish dinner:** **Heaneys** in Canton/Pontcanna — seasonal modern cooking from chef Tommy Heaney, with a strong cocktail/bar setup and tasting menus. ([guide.michelin.com](https://guide.michelin.com/gb/en/south-glamorgan/cardiff/restaurant/heaneys?utm_source=openai))  
- **Great Indian food:** **Purple Poppadom** on Cowbridge Road East — Michelin-listed, known for polished Indian cooking and signature dishes like tiffin sea bass. ([guide.michelin.com](https://guide.michelin.com/gb/en/south-glamorgan/cardiff/restaurant/purple-poppadom?utm_source=openai))  
- **Casual but excellent:** **Matsudai Ramen** in Grangetown — a well-liked ramen spot and one of Time Out’s top Cardiff choices. ([timeout.com](https://www.timeout.com/cardiff/restaurants/best-restaurants-in-cardiff))  
- **Spanish / steak / tapas near the centre:** **Asador 44**, **Bar 44**, and **Curado** around Westgate Street — Visit Wales describes this area as Cardiff’s “Spanish Triangle,” good for tapas, wine, and a more sociable meal. ([visitwales.com](https://www.visitwales.com/things-do/food-and-drink/places-eat-and-drink/guide-eating-cardiff))  
- **Neighbourhood bistro:** **Milkwood** in Pontcanna — relaxed, seasonal, modern Welsh-style food. ([visitwales.com](https://www.visitwales.com/things-do/food-and-drink/places-eat-and-drink/guide-eating-cardiff))  

**Best areas to look:**  
- **Pontcanna / Canton** for some of Cardiff’s best independent restaurants.  
- **City centre / Westgate Street** for Spanish food, steak, tapas, and pre-event meals near Principality Stadium.  
- **City Road, Roath** for a wide range of international food. ([visitwales.com](https://www.visitwales.com/things-do/food-and-drink/places-eat-and-drink/guide-eating-cardiff))  

If you want one safe recommendation: **book Gorse for a special meal**, or **Heaneys** if you want fine dining that’s a little more relaxed.

Assuming you mean **Cardiff, Wales**: yes — I’d pick **Gorse in Pontcanna** if you want a properly special meal. It’s a small, intimate restaurant focused on seasonal Welsh produce, and Visit Wales notes it holds a **Michelin Star** and **three AA Rosettes**. ([visitwales.com](https://www.visitwales.com/things-do/food-and-drink/places-eat-and-drink/guide-eating-cardiff))

A few good alternatives:

- **Heaneys** — relaxed but high-end, good for tasting menus or Sunday lunch; it’s run by Tommy Heaney and is a strong all-round choice. ([visitwales.com](https://www.visitwales.com/things-do/food-and-drink/places-eat-and-drink/guide-eating-cardiff))  
- **Asador 44** — great if you like Spanish food, grilled meats, steak, seafood, and wine; very good for a special but less “formal” dinner. ([visitwales.com](https://www.visitwales.com/things-do/food-and-drink/places-eat-and-drink/guide-eating-cardiff))  
- **Hiraeth** — inventive Welsh-focused cooking near Victoria Park; good if you want something local and creative. ([visitwales.com](https://www.visitwales.com/things-do/food-and-drink/places-eat-and-drink/guide-eating-cardiff))  
- **Ffwrnes Pizza** — more casual/cheap: wood-fired pizza inside Cardiff Central Market. ([thegoodfoodguide.co.uk](https://www.thegoodfoodguide.co.uk/where-to-eat/best-restaurants-in-cardiff))  

**My quick recommendation:**  
- **Best special occasion:** Gorse  
- **Best reliable nice dinner:** Heaneys  
- **Best steak/grill:** Asador 44  
- **Best casual bite:** Ffwrnes Pizza  

Book ahead for Gorse, Heaneys, and Asador 44.
